notes: Core Dell|EMC CX product family deployment architecture; expanding from data center to workgroup.
Dell PowerVault NAS family: 775N, 770N, 745N; file-oriented data center and entry-level solutions.
Primary disk array interface for Dell|EMC CX product family; online pool storage for highest-performance workloads.
ATA drives used in Dell|EMC AX100 (up to 3 TB SATA); midline/nearline storage at lower cost than FC. SAS/SATA mixed backplanes became universal in enterprise servers.
ATA/SATA disks for fixed content and nearline pool; emerging major buyer trend per Aberdeen research. Proved correct — SATA became 85% of enterprise data by 2011.
Third generation: CX300 (workgroup), CX500 (department), CX700 (data center); replaced CX200/CX400/CX600.
Entry-level SATA SAN; up to 3 TB; wizard-based setup; dual controllers; mirrored cache; Brocade SAN switch optional. Validated midline concept at workgroup level.
PowerVault 775N, 770N (midrange file servers), 745N (entry-level); Windows Storage Server 2003 based.
NAS OS chosen by Dell over proprietary NAS OS; inherits Advanced Server features including Active Directory and clustering.
Point-in-time copy software for entire CX family (CX300 through CX700); availability from entry level is key differentiator.
Mirroring/data replication software for CX product family.
Device management software for the CX storage product family.
SAN management software; web-based UI for discovering
Enterprise backup/recovery software offered by Dell; featured in enterprise data center backup portfolio.
Dell standardized on LTO-2 for PowerVault 160T enterprise tape library; LTO family forward-compatible.
PowerVault 122 tape autoloader; 132T and 136T libraries; 160T enterprise library. DLT and LTO derivatives.
Core Dell business model applied to storage; enables superior price/performance through manufacturing efficiency.
Dell practice of bundling multiple storage software components to reduce SKU complexity and deployment friction.
